vocabulary.englishMeaning: Fluent or persuasive in speaking or writing.
exampleSentences: His eloquent speech moved the clients.
vocabulary.commonMistakes: Spelled as eloquant with an "a" sometimes. It is always ending with "ent".
vocabulary.memoryTrick: Elephant speech - an elephant speaking so beautifully that everyone listens.
vocabulary.realLifeUsage: Used to describe presenters, public speakers, or well-written pitch documents.

vocabulary.englishMeaning: The capacity to recover quickly from difficulties.
exampleSentences: The engineering team showed great resilience during the server outage.
Nylon is notable for its resilience.
vocabulary.commonMistakes: Spelled as resiliance with an "a". It is always ending with "ence".
vocabulary.memoryTrick: Sounds like re-silence. When obstacles make noise, you recover quickly to quiet silence.
vocabulary.realLifeUsage: Used in team retrospectives, HR reviews, and psychological growth seminars.

vocabulary.englishMeaning: Dealing with things realistically based on practical considerations.
exampleSentences: We need a pragmatic solution to this bottleneck, not a theoretical framework.
vocabulary.commonMistakes: Do not confuse with dogmatic (strict/stubborn belief).
vocabulary.memoryTrick: Pragmatic sounds like practical magic. Practical actions work like magic.
vocabulary.realLifeUsage: Commonly used in scoping technical requirements, budgeting, and planning sprints.

vocabulary.englishMeaning: Delay or postpone action; put off doing something.
exampleSentences: Do not procrastinate on writing the test cases.
vocabulary.commonMistakes: Often misspelled as procrastnate. Do not forget the "i" after the "t".
vocabulary.memoryTrick: Pro at putting things off until tomorrow (crast/crust in Latin).
vocabulary.realLifeUsage: Used when discussing study plans, deliverables, and team productivity.

vocabulary.englishMeaning: Showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise.
exampleSentences: Our QA team is meticulous in finding edge-case bugs.
He kept meticulous records of files.
vocabulary.commonMistakes: Pronounced as meti-cu-lous. Avoid writing meticules.
vocabulary.memoryTrick: Meticulous has "tick". Check off every item with a "tick" because you are careful.
vocabulary.realLifeUsage: Used in performance reviews, code styling standards, and accounting audits.

vocabulary.englishMeaning: Work jointly on an activity or project.
exampleSentences: We need to collaborate with the design team.
They collaborated on the brand release.
vocabulary.commonMistakes: Double "l" and single "r" - collaborate. Do not spell as colaborate.
vocabulary.memoryTrick: Co-Labor-ate. Jointly laboring or working together to achieve a goal.
vocabulary.realLifeUsage: Common in remote work setups, meeting scripts, and task cards.

vocabulary.englishMeaning: Having or showing a confident and forceful personality.
exampleSentences: Be assertive when negotiating your career salary.
vocabulary.commonMistakes: Do not confuse with aggressive. Assertive is confident; aggressive is hostile.
vocabulary.memoryTrick: Assert. Assuring your position with confidence.
vocabulary.realLifeUsage: Common in self-growth programs, interview drills, and corporate leadership coaching.

vocabulary.englishMeaning: Having or showing the ability to speak fluently and coherently.
exampleSentences: An articulate engineer explains complex designs clearly.
vocabulary.commonMistakes: Avoid writing articulat without the ending "e".
vocabulary.memoryTrick: Article-ate. Speaking so clearly it could be written directly as a news article.
vocabulary.realLifeUsage: Used in speech evaluations, customer pitches, and manager reviews.

vocabulary.englishMeaning: Including all or nearly all elements or aspects of something.
exampleSentences: We ran a comprehensive audit of the backend server.
vocabulary.commonMistakes: Do not confuse with comprehensible (meaning easy to understand).
vocabulary.memoryTrick: Comprehend everything. Covers all areas completely.
vocabulary.realLifeUsage: Common in software audits, textbooks, and syllabus structures.

vocabulary.englishMeaning: Make (suffering, deficiency, or a problem) less severe.
exampleSentences: Caching will alleviate the query pressure on the database.
vocabulary.commonMistakes: Double "l" and single "v" - alleviate. Not aleveate.
vocabulary.memoryTrick: Sounds like elevation. Elevating your pain to rise above it and make it less severe.
vocabulary.realLifeUsage: Used in system optimization scoping, healthcare, and customer conflict resolution.

grammar.explanation: In English, words are categorized into eight primary parts of speech based on their function in a sentence: Nouns, Pronouns, Verbs, Adjectives, Adverbs, Prepositions, Conjunctions, and Interjections.
examples: Adjective: The project has a "clean" interface.

interactiveQuiz.question: Identify the noun in this sentence: "The developer finished the product."
interactiveQuiz.explanation: Product is a noun (thing). Developer is also a noun, but not an option.

interactiveQuiz.question: What part of speech is the word "quickly" in: "She solved the query quickly"?
interactiveQuiz.explanation: Quickly is an adverb describing how the action (solved) was performed.

speaking.prompt: Introduce yourself as a software professional joining a team meeting.
sentencesToRead: Hello everyone, I am delighted to collaborate with you.
I bring expertise in backend scoping and database structures.
I look forward to hitting our upcoming project milestones.

listening.audioPrompt: A short monologue detailing a project sprint kickoff.
listening.transcript: Good morning team. Today we kickoff our sprint. Our focus is to alleviate page loading times by implementing custom caching. We need to be meticulous with review sessions to guarantee zero leaks. Let us collaborate and succeed.

fillInBlanks.question: Our focus is to alleviate ____ times.

multipleChoice.question: What optimization is proposed to speed up page loading times?

writing.prompt: Write an email introduction introducing yourself to a new overseas client.
writing.placeholder:
Dear Client,

I hope this email finds you well. My name is...

quiz.question: What is a synonym of "Eloquent"?
quiz.explanation: Eloquent means fluent/persuasive, making Articulate the synonym.

quiz.question: Select the antonym for "Resilience":
quiz.explanation: Resilience is durability/grit; Fragility is weakness/vulnerability.

quiz.question: Which word is a verb?
quiz.explanation: Alleviate is an action (verb). The others are adjectives and nouns.

quiz.question: In the sentence "He is an eloquent speaker," what is the word "speaker"?
Based on the listening transcript, what does the team want to ensure is at zero?
quiz.explanation: The transcript says: "We need to be meticulous with review sessions to guarantee zero leaks."
